summary: Plan + dispatch contract + investigation are internally consistent and the major reframes are accurate. ZERO build failures CONFIRMED. 276 runtime test failures CONFIRMED EXACTLY. 4 doctest failures CONFIRMED with verified file:line + working fix sketches. 17 archived subcommands CONFIRMED. 33 vuln_migration_v1_red Cat-B carry-forwards CONFIRMED with no scope overlap into M3/M4. 26 + 1 (doctest) = 27 binaries with FAILED summary CONFIRMED. The plan correctly reduces the M6 charter from a 26-build-failure crisis into a hygiene milestone, and the M3 ATOMIC bulk-delete pattern correctly mirrors vuln-migration-v1 M5. THREE non-blocking observations and ONE near-blocker classification-ambiguity (search command) are documented below; none rise to the BLOCKER level given the plan's M1 RED-capture gating discipline. Dispatch is APPROVED conditional on the worker treating the M1 classification of `search` invocations as ACTIVE (mapping to SmartSearch via #[command(name="search")]) rather than archived.

tigers_and_elephants.name: search-CLI-string-classification-ambiguity
tigers_and_elephants.description: The Subcommand enum has `SmartSearch(SmartSearchArgs)` annotated with `#[command(name = "search")]` at crates/tldr-cli/src/main.rs:141-142. So `tldr search ...` is an ACTIVE CLI invocation that maps to SmartSearch. But there is also `crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/archived/search.rs`, and the dispatch contract M1 enumeration mandate explicitly lists `search` in the candidate archived-subcmd set: "<archived-subcmd> is in the set {cfg, dfg, ssa, gvn, alias, dominators, live_vars, abstract_interp, arch, behavioral, bounds, diff_impact, equivalence, maintainability, mutability, purity, search, secrets}". A literal-reading worker following the dispatch contract would enumerate every `cmd.args(["search", ...])` invocation as archived → DELETE in M3. Verified `args(["search"...])` invocations: cli_tests.rs (2 sites — "find_me", "[invalid"), cli_search_context_tests.rs (3+ sites — "test", "--help"). These tests target the ACTIVE SmartSearch command. The plan body §M3 acknowledges this at L128 ("search is a borderline case — there's a search_* family that may be partly active; M1 enumeration must classify"). However the dispatch contract M1 mandate normalizes `search` into the archived candidate set without disambiguation language. RISK: M3 atomic deletion may delete active SmartSearch tests, leaving SmartSearch (an active feature) without test coverage AND introducing test-count drift the M4 reconciliation gate (failure-count = 33) will pass blindly because deleting a passing test does not change the failure count.

verification.passing_search_tests_at_head: cli_tests.rs: 2 sites; cli_search_context_tests.rs: 5+ sites. Most are passing (not in /tmp/wti-test.txt failed-test list, except a small set targeting old archived behavior).

tigers_and_elephants.mitigation_required_pre_dispatch: The dispatch worker prompt MUST instruct M1 enumeration to use CLI-NAME-after-clap-rename (e.g., `search` maps to SmartSearch which IS active) as the source of truth, NOT module presence in archived/. Recommended action: add an explicit M1 disambiguation rule: 'If cmd.args(["<X>", ...]) where X resolves via clap-name (#[command(name="X")] or lowercased-variant) to a present Subcommand variant, the test is ACTIVE and PRESERVED. Module presence in archived/ is informational, NOT decisive.' Without this clarification, the M3 ATOMIC commit risks unintended deletion of SmartSearch coverage.

tigers_and_elephants.name: Cat-A-count-estimate-may-undershoot
tigers_and_elephants.description: Plan and contract estimate Cat-A (archived-cmd tests) at "approximately 200" entries. Live failure breakdown: 276 (total runtime) - 33 (Cat-B) - ~17 canonical Cat-C - ~5 newly-observed Cat-C ≈ 221 remaining = Cat-A. That's ~10% above the "~200" estimate. The dispatch contract's M1 numerical reconciliation tolerance is `+/- 5 drift` (mandate: "|Cat-A| + 33 + |Cat-C| + 4 = 276 +/- 5"). 221 + 33 + 17 + 4 = 275; 221 + 33 + 22 + 4 = 280. So the +/- 5 envelope is right at the edge — but is satisfied. RISK: If M1 enumeration finds Cat-A=205 (matching the estimate) but live failures are 221, the worker may incorrectly conclude the enumeration is INCOMPLETE and re-walk. The contract mandate is correct in its envelope; the plan-body text "~200" undersells.
tigers_and_elephants.mitigation: Plan's +/- 5 drift gate is appropriate; worker should NOT panic at Cat-A=220. Update: the plan's CHANGELOG draft at §6 says "~200" — for accuracy in M5, the actual count should come from `git diff --stat` of the M3 commit (per R5/R7 mandate) rather than be reused from the plan estimate.

tigers_and_elephants.name: P2_multilang_tests-mixed-archived-active
tigers_and_elephants.description: p2_multilang_tests.rs contains 45 test functions across 5 commands × multiple langs (gvn, bounds, resources, contracts, behavioral). Of these, gvn (archived) and bounds (archived) test rows must be deleted; resources (active), contracts (active), behavioral (active) test rows must be preserved. Live capture shows 37 failed of 45 in this binary — implying ~8 passing tests are the active subset. Plan §M3 says "delete archived <cmd>_<lang> matrix subset — bulk of 37 failures; preserve active subset" which is correct in principle. But this is one of the higher-risk M3 deletions because the boundary is intra-file. Same risk applies to exhaustive_matrix.rs (729 tests, 53 failed; 676 to preserve).
tigers_and_elephants.mitigation: M1 enumeration is mechanical (CLI-string match against active set), so even mixed files are safely classifiable. The cargo build + clippy stop-thresholds catch orphan helpers. The atomic-rollback rule is the safety net.

non_blocking_observations.name: L172-IGNORE-hedge-language-conflicts-with-dispatch-mandate
non_blocking_observations.description: Plan §4 line 172 says: 'val013_daemon_status_cross_cwd_test.rs (daemon_status_from_other_cwd_reports_running): Investigate; may be order-dependent — if so, fix the daemon status check; if cleanly-flaky, IGNORE is acceptable per no-gaming rule (only environmental dependencies qualify) | FIX TBD'. This phrasing suggests IGNORE is a fallback. The dispatch contract `no_test_gaming_rule` mandate at L12 is unambiguous: 'ZERO #[ignore] markers permitted in this milestone'. Plan §5 L253 also says 'Total IGNORE count: 0'. The L172 hedge should be removed in the worker prompt, replacing it with: 'order-dependent → FIX the order-dependence; environmental → ESCALATE to operator (out-of-scope), not IGNORE'.
non_blocking_observations.action_required: Worker prompt should reaffirm zero #[ignore] policy; if val013 turns out to be genuinely flaky, treat as escalation (operator-escalation-regression.json) not IGNORE.

non_blocking_observations.name: M3-stop-threshold-arithmetic-includes-only-Cat-A-in-drop
non_blocking_observations.description: M3 stop-threshold L143 says: 'cargo test ... failure count drops by ~200 (Cat-A failures eliminated; remaining failures = Cat-B + Cat-C + 4 doctests + 0 = ~33 + 16-25 + 4 = ~53-62 expected)'. But M2 (doctests) lands BEFORE M3 (both depend on M1; per dispatch wave numbers M2 is wave 2, M3 is wave 3). So when M3 runs, the 4 doctests are ALREADY GREEN (assuming M2 ran first). The M3 stop-threshold remaining-count formula correctly reflects this with '+ 4 doctests + 0 = ...' (zeroed). This is correct, just mildly opaque.

non_blocking_observations.name: M2-and-M3-both-depend-on-M1-but-can-run-in-any-order
non_blocking_observations.description: Per dispatch contract milestones list: M2 depends:[M1], M3 depends:[M1] (NOT M2). So M2 + M3 can run in parallel after M1. But the plan's wave numbers (M2=wave2, M3=wave3) imply sequential. Doesn't matter for correctness — both are independent test-only changes, no source-code conflict — but worker should be told whether to run M2+M3 sequentially (per wave-numbers) or in parallel (per depends-on graph).
non_blocking_observations.action_required: Worker prompt should default to wave-order (sequential): M1 → M2 → M3 → M4 → M5. Parallelizing M2/M3 is not worth the orchestration complexity.

non_blocking_observations.name: search-command-name-collision-in-archived-list
non_blocking_observations.description: Per ELEPHANT-1: dispatch contract M1 mandate puts 'search' in the archived candidate set, but `tldr search` IS the active CLI alias for SmartSearch. The worker prompt must explicitly clarify this. Repeated here as a non-blocking observation in case ELEPHANT-1 is overlooked.

claim_1_zero_build_failures.verification: Ran cargo build --workspace --tests --features semantic at HEAD c14e440. Result: 'Finished `dev` profile in 0.62s' exit=0. Plan's reframe is CORRECT; M6's '26 build failures' was indeed a category mislabel for '26 binaries with at least one runtime test failure'.

claim_2_276_runtime_failures.verification: grep -cE '^test .* FAILED$' /tmp/wti-test.txt → 276 (exact match). 'test result: FAILED' summary lines → 27 (26 runtime binaries + 1 doctest). Sum of '<N> failed' across summary lines → 276 (exact match). Live capture file timestamp matches the planning loop's RED capture.

claim_3_17_archived_subcommands.verification: ls of crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/archived/: abstract_interp.rs, alias.rs, arch.rs, behavioral.rs, bounds.rs, cfg.rs, dfg.rs, diff_impact.rs, dominators.rs, equivalence.rs, live_vars.rs, maintainability.rs, mutability.rs, purity.rs, search.rs, secrets.rs, ssa.rs = 17 files.

claim_4_subcommand_enum_excludes_archive.claim: main.rs Subcommand enum has NO Cfg/Dfg/Ssa/etc variants
claim_4_subcommand_enum_excludes_archive.verification: grep on main.rs Subcommand-variant patterns → no matches for archived names. Active variants confirmed: Tree, Structure, Calls, Impact, Dead, ReachingDefs, Taint, Available, Slice, SmartSearch (search), Context, Smells, Extract, Imports, Importers, Complexity, Churn, Debt, Health, Hubs, Whatbreaks, Patterns, Inheritance, ChangeImpact, Deps, Diagnostics, Doctor, References, Clones, Dice, Loc, Cognitive, Halstead, Coverage, Hotspots, Embed, Semantic, Similar, Contracts, DeadStores, Chop, Specs, Invariants, Verify, Cohesion, Temporal, Resources, Coupling, Interface, Explain, Todo, Definition, Diff, ApiCheck, Vuln. Archived names absent. NUANCE: Search is renamed via #[command(name="search")] from SmartSearch — see ELEPHANT-1.

claim_5_doctest_funcindexproxy_at_line_1.claim: FuncIndexProxy::insert at L1109 is unimplemented!() stub; FuncIndexProxyMut at L1147 is the working impl; doctest at L1025-1037 calls FuncIndexProxy::insert.
claim_5_doctest_funcindexproxy_at_line_1.verification: Read crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/cross_file_types.rs:1020-1230. Doctest at L1027 (annotated 'use tldr_core::callgraph::cross_file_types::FuncIndexProxy; ...; let mut index = FuncIndexProxy::new(interner); index.insert(...);'). Confirmed insert at L1068-L1109 ends with `unimplemented!("FuncIndexProxy::insert requires mutable interner - see FuncIndexProxyMut")`. FuncIndexProxyMut at L1147 has working insert at L1171 + working get at L1179. Plan fix sketch (`let mut index = FuncIndexProxyMut::new(); index.insert("mymodule", "my_func", "src/mymodule.py"); assert_eq!(index.get("mymodule", "my_func"), Some("src/mymodule.py"));`) compiles and is consistent with the actual API.

claim_6_kotlin_doctest_bare_fence_at_L74.claim: Bare ``` at L74 enclosing pseudo-grammar block
claim_6_kotlin_doctest_bare_fence_at_L74.verification: Read crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/languages/kotlin.rs:71-85. L74 is bare ``` opening a pseudo-grammar block at L75-L84, closed by ``` at L84. Rustdoc treats unannotated as `rust` and tries to compile, hence the failure. Fix: change L74 ``` to ```text. ONE-LINE EDIT correct.

claim_7_luau_doctest_bare_fence_at_L217.claim: Bare ``` at L217 enclosing pseudo-grammar block
claim_7_luau_doctest_bare_fence_at_L217.verification: Read crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/languages/luau.rs:215-230. L217 is bare ``` opening pseudo-grammar block at L218-L222, closed by ``` at L223. Same fix shape as kotlin.

claim_8_surface_triggers_stale_contracts.claim: Doctest at L15 uses `tldr_core::contracts::triggers::extract_name_triggers` but function is at `tldr_core::surface::triggers::extract_name_triggers`
claim_8_surface_triggers_stale_contracts.verification: Read crates/tldr-core/src/surface/triggers.rs:1-22. Doctest at L16 indeed says `use tldr_core::contracts::triggers::extract_name_triggers;`. Function `pub fn extract_name_triggers` is at L22 of the same file (within `surface::triggers` module). Verified `pub mod surface;` exists in crates/tldr-core/src/lib.rs but `pub mod contracts;` does NOT (no top-level contracts module published). The actual doctest error is `error: could not find 'contracts' in 'tldr_core'` — confirming the path is invalid. Fix: change to `use tldr_core::surface::triggers::extract_name_triggers;`. CORRECT.

claim_9_kotlin_swift_supported_per_parse.claim: parser.rs:136-137 has tree_sitter_kotlin_ng (Kotlin) and tree_sitter_swift (Swift) — both languages are SUPPORTED.
claim_9_kotlin_swift_supported_per_parse.verification: Read parser.rs:130-138. L136: `TldrLanguage::Kotlin => Some(tree_sitter_kotlin_ng::LANGUAGE.into())`. L137: `TldrLanguage::Swift => Some(tree_sitter_swift::LANGUAGE.into())`. Test_kotlin_returns_unsupported and test_swift_returns_unsupported at language_parity_test.rs:1928,1940 are stale; preserved comment at L1922-23 explicitly notes 'Their corresponding test_*_returns_unsupported tests have been removed'. DELETE-on-stale is correct disposition.

claim_10_test_surface_unsupported_langua.claim: bench_surface_search_multilang.rs:876 calls extract_api_surface(..., Some("ruby"), ...) but ruby IS supported
claim_10_test_surface_unsupported_langua.verification: Read bench_surface_search_multilang.rs:870-893. L882: `Some("ruby")`. Investigation cites surface/mod.rs:90-118 as the match arm where Ruby is handled. Plan fix sketch (switch to 'haskell' or another genuinely-unsupported language) is correct. PASS.

claim_11_tree_handles_empty_directory_fi.claim: ast_tests.rs:80 uses fixtures_dir().join('empty-dir') but the directory is missing on disk
claim_11_tree_handles_empty_directory_fi.verification: Read ast_tests.rs:79-91. ls of crates/tldr-core/tests/fixtures/: extractor, fix, python-project, simple-project, surface, typescript-project — NO 'empty-dir' folder. Plan fix (create fixtures/empty-dir/.gitkeep) is correct.

claim_12_val010_change_impact_test_self_.claim: val010_change_impact_git_path_test.rs:141 (detection_error_hints_at_origin_branch_when_only_remote_exists) is FIXable with self-init tempdir + git update-ref; no real network.
claim_12_val010_change_impact_test_self_.verification: Read val010 lines 130-200. L146: init_git_repo_with_one_commit(project) — self-init. L161: git update-ref refs/remotes/origin/feature-x — local. L199: assert reason.contains('origin/feature-x'). FIX path requires updating change_impact_extended's NoBaseline reason builder to include the origin/<branch> hint. ENVIRONMENTAL DEPENDENCIES: NONE. FIX disposition correct.

claim_13_vuln_migration_v1_red_33_failur.claim: 33 vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forwards in vuln_migration_v1_red.rs are excluded from M3/M4 scope
claim_13_vuln_migration_v1_red_33_failur.verification: test result: FAILED. 133 passed; 33 failed (matches investigation.json count exactly). Plan §1.OUT-OF-SCOPE explicit. Dispatch contract `category_b_out_of_scope` mandate explicit. M3 preserved-list explicitly includes 'All vuln_migration_v1_red.rs entries (Category-B; out-of-scope)'. NO scope overlap.

claim_14_no_ignore_markers.claim: ZERO #[ignore] markers in this milestone's dispositions
claim_14_no_ignore_markers.verification: Plan §5 L253: 'Total IGNORE count: 0.' Dispatch contract `no_test_gaming_rule`: 'ZERO #[ignore] markers permitted'. Caveat: plan §4 L172 hedges 'IGNORE is acceptable... if cleanly-flaky' for val013 — see OBS-1 (low-severity inconsistency).

claim_15_ssa_cli_tests_full_delete_appro.claim: ssa_cli_tests.rs has 26 tests, all targeting archived ssa — DELETE entire file
claim_15_ssa_cli_tests_full_delete_appro.verification: grep -cE '^#\[test\]' on ssa_cli_tests.rs → 26. Sample of args invocations: all target 'ssa'. Live failure count 26 matches. Whole-file delete is correct.

claim_16_gvn_cli_tests_full_delete_appro.claim: gvn_cli_tests.rs has 9 tests, all targeting archived-equivalent gvn — DELETE entire file
claim_16_gvn_cli_tests_full_delete_appro.verification: 9 tests, 9 failures. gvn is not in main.rs Subcommand enum and not in archived/ directory but is archived-equivalent (no active CLI). Whole-file delete is correct (no orthogonal-real among them).

claim_17_search_subcmd_name_collision.claim: `tldr search` is active (SmartSearch via #[command(name="search")]), but search is also archived
claim_17_search_subcmd_name_collision.verification: main.rs L141-142: `#[command(name = "search")] SmartSearch(SmartSearchArgs)`. Active: yes. archived/search.rs exists. Tests: cli_tests.rs has 2 sites with `args(["search", "find_me", ...])`; cli_search_context_tests.rs has 5+ sites. These target ACTIVE SmartSearch. ELEPHANT-1: dispatch contract M1 mandate puts 'search' in archived candidate set, creating misclassification risk.

risk_register.name: M1-search-misclassification-deletes-active-SmartSearch-tests
risk_register.trigger: M1 worker mechanically applies the contract's archived candidate set including 'search', enumerates SmartSearch tests as Cat-A, M3 ATOMIC commit deletes them.
risk_register.mitigation: Worker prompt MUST be amended to clarify: classification is by CLI-name-after-clap-rename (the string the user types), and that name must NOT correspond to any active Subcommand variant (after applying #[command(name=...)] renames). archived/ module presence is informational, NOT decisive.
risk_register.detection: Post-M3, cargo test --workspace --features semantic --no-fail-fast --release should show 33 vuln + 16-25 Cat-C remaining failures. If failure count drops below 50 (suggesting NEW formerly-passing tests deleted), or above 60 (suggesting deletion incomplete), investigate.

risk_register.name: M3-orphan-helper-imports-not-caught-at-planning
risk_register.trigger: M3 deletes test functions that referenced helper functions also used by other tests; the helper becomes unused but only via a dead-code warning surface.
risk_register.mitigation: M3 stop-threshold gates on cargo build --workspace --tests + cargo clippy -D warnings — both will surface orphans. Plan's atomic-rollback rule requires REVERT entire M3 commit if either fails.

risk_register.name: Cat-C-fix-cascades-introduce-new-failures
risk_register.trigger: Fixing git_log empty-repo behavior (Ok empty vs Err) might break a positive-case test elsewhere. Same for cognitive-else, parse_coverage empty-handling, etc.
risk_register.mitigation: Plan mandates per-fix verification (M4-fix-by-fix-capture.json) — after each fix, re-run cargo test and confirm delta = -1 (one RED→GREEN, no GREEN→RED). Reverts the fix if collateral damage observed.

risk_register.name: Cat-C-test-realistic-python-root-cause-unverified
risk_register.trigger: investigation.json marks test_realistic_python_project_structure as ? INFERRED, no live root-cause analysis. Plan says 'numeric/semantic alignment' which is hand-waving.
risk_register.mitigation: M1 RED capture requires reading each Cat-C test's actual stdout/stderr; if test_realistic_python_project_structure turns out to be a non-trivial regression, escalate per R5/operator-escalation-regression.json. Plan correctly hedges with 'FIX TBD'.
risk_register.detection: M1 RED capture analysis is the gate.

risk_register.name: M5-CHANGELOG-numeric-drift
risk_register.trigger: M5 CHANGELOG draft says '~200 obsolete CLI integration tests' — but actual count may be ~218-225. If M5 worker copies the draft verbatim instead of sourcing from git diff --stat, public-facing artifact misstates scope.
risk_register.mitigation: Plan §5/§7-R5 mandates CHANGELOG counts come from `git diff --stat HEAD~N HEAD`, not manual estimation. Worker should be reminded.
risk_register.detection: Visual review of M5 CHANGELOG entry against M3 commit stats.

risk_register.name: 276-failure-baseline-may-drift-between-M1-RED-capture-and-M4-final-verify
risk_register.trigger: Daemon-related failures (val013, val006) are sometimes order-dependent; live capture taken on wall-clock day X may show 276; M4 final verify on day Y may show 275 or 277.
risk_register.mitigation: Plan's +/- 5 drift envelope on the M1 reconciliation gate accommodates this. M4 final stop-threshold is failure-count = 33 EXACTLY (not 33 +/- 5) which is stricter — if 1 daemon test is flaky, M4 may BLOCK on 34. This is a real edge case.
risk_register.detection: If M4 final shows 34 with the 1 extra failure being a daemon-status test, worker should escalate (via daemon_test fix or re-run) rather than declare BLOCKED.

M1.rationale: Pure investigation + artifact production. ZERO source/test changes. The reconciliation gate (Cat-A + 33 + Cat-C + 4 = 276 +/- 5) is well-defined. M1 produces all artifacts that gate M2/M3/M4 — appropriate gating.
preconditions: Worker prompt MUST clarify 'search' classification per ELEPHANT-1 before M1 enumeration starts (else mis-enumeration cascades into M3 atomic delete).

M2.rationale: 4 surgical doctest edits. Each verified at exact file:line. Each fix sketch reviewed and confirmed compilable. ONE-LINE edits for kotlin/luau/triggers; small rewrite for FuncIndexProxy doctest using FuncIndexProxyMut (verified API).

M3.rationale: ATOMIC bulk-delete. Mirrors successful vuln-migration-v1 M5 pattern. cargo build + clippy -D warnings stop-thresholds catch orphans. Atomic rollback rule preserves recovery path.
preconditions: M1 enumeration must be VERIFIED COMPLETE before M3 starts (every Cat-A entry catalogued; CLI-name disambiguation applied per ELEPHANT-1).
M2 must have landed (so doctest count is 0 in failure baseline).

M4.rationale: Targeted Cat-C fixes. Per-fix verification reduces cascade risk. The 16-25 Cat-C entries have well-understood root causes (5 VERIFIED at file:line; ~10 INFERRED but bounded scope).
preconditions: M3 must have landed (Cat-A residue cleared; failure baseline reduced to ~53).
If a Cat-C root cause turns out to be a regression from a prior milestone (regex/FAI/sanitizer/vuln), worker MUST escalate via reports/operator-escalation-regression.json — DO NOT silently fix.
If failure-count > 33 after M4: BLOCKED — root-cause additional residue.

M5.rationale: Documentation-only. CHANGELOG entry sourced from M3+M4 git diff --stat (not estimated). Local annotated tag only (no push, no publish, no version bump).

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: M1-search-disambiguation-spot-check
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: Before M1 enumeration completes, verify that NO test invocation of `args(["search", ...])` is in the Cat-A enumeration JSON. Spot-check by grep'ing M1-archived-cmd-test-enumeration.json for 'archived_subcommand': 'search'. If any match exists, M1 is mis-enumerating active SmartSearch tests; STOP and re-enumerate.

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: post-M3-failure-count-bracket-check
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: After M3 ATOMIC commit, run cargo test --workspace --features semantic --no-fail-fast --release | grep -c '^test .* FAILED$'. Expected: ~50-60 (33 Cat-B + 16-25 Cat-C + 0 doctest). If count < 45, suspect ELEPHANT-1 (active tests deleted). If count > 65, M3 deletion incomplete or new regression introduced. Either case: investigate before M4.

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: post-M3-clippy-orphan-helper-check
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: cargo clippy --workspace --tests --features semantic -- -D warnings — must exit 0. Catches orphan test helpers post-deletion. Built into M3 stop-threshold.

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: M4-per-fix-failure-delta-check
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: After EACH Cat-C fix in M4, re-run cargo test and confirm exactly 1 RED→GREEN with 0 GREEN→RED. If delta != -1, revert the fix and re-investigate (per PR-3 mitigation).

discriminative_tests_recommended.name: M5-changelog-count-vs-actual-diff-check
discriminative_tests_recommended.purpose: Before committing M5 CHANGELOG entry, run `git diff --stat HEAD~3 HEAD -- crates/tldr-cli/tests/ crates/tldr-core/tests/` (or equivalent boundary). Confirm the LOC delta numbers in CHANGELOG match the diff-stat output. Per PR-5.

dispatch_readiness.verdict: GO with one MANDATORY worker-prompt addendum
dispatch_readiness.mandatory_addendum_before_dispatch: Worker prompt MUST include the following M1 classification disambiguation: 'For each `cmd.args(["<X>", ...])` test invocation, treat <X> as ACTIVE if and only if it matches a Subcommand variant in main.rs after applying #[command(name="...")] renames. This means: `search` is ACTIVE (maps to SmartSearch via #[command(name="search")] at main.rs:141-142). Module presence in crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/archived/ is INFORMATIONAL ONLY, NOT decisive. The dispatch contract M1 mandate's archived candidate set lists `search` for completeness of the historical archive, but the worker MUST exclude it from Cat-A classification because the active CLI alias takes precedence.'
secondary_recommendations: Update OBS-1 (val013 IGNORE hedge): Replace plan §4 L172 'IGNORE is acceptable... if cleanly-flaky' phrasing with FIX-or-ESCALATE in the worker prompt. Zero #[ignore] policy is the dispatch-contract mandate.

Update PR-6 awareness: M4 final verify against 33 EXACTLY may be fragile if val013 (or another daemon test) flakes. Recommend two-of-three runs to confirm baseline before declaring failure-count = 33.
Reaffirm Cargo.lock guard before each commit (regex/FAI/sanitizer/vuln milestones all dirty Cargo.lock; this milestone must NOT stage).
escalation_triggers_during_run: If post-M3 failure-count is outside [45, 65]: investigate ELEPHANT-1 over-deletion or under-deletion before M4.
If any Cat-C root cause traces to a regression in regex/FAI/sanitizer/vuln milestones: STOP and escalate via reports/operator-escalation-regression.json.
If cargo clippy -D warnings shows orphan helpers post-M3 that cannot be cleanly deleted: rollback M3, re-enumerate.
If failure-count > 33 after M4 and additional residue is real (not flake): root-cause; do NOT silently proceed to M5.

verification_files_examined: /tmp/wti-test.txt (live RED capture from planning loop) — cross-reference
